message:
test(readme): cover 17 more StringAccessor methods (fd90.247 / br-15vsk)

Extends readme_string_accessor_compiles_and_runs to cover 17 more
StringAccessor methods. Combined with fd90.194's 26 methods, ~43 of
the 50+ methods documented in README lines 439-453 are now exercised.

Case (rounds out the 6-cell category):
- casefold, swapcase

Search positions:
- find, rfind, index_of, rindex_of

Predicates (rounds out the 9-cell category):
- isalnum, isdecimal, islower, isnumeric, isspace, istitle, isupper

Other:
- cat("|") — concat across rows → single String
- lstrip_chars(" \t") — strip explicit chars
- rjust(10, ' ') — right-justify pad
- partition("-") / rpartition("-") — return (before, sep, after) Series tuples
- normalize("NFC") — Unicode normal-form conversion
- count("o") — count literal occurrences
- extractall(regex) — DataFrame of regex captures across all matches

Verified: 35 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"frankenpandas-126io","title":"fd90.151: bump fp-frame test count 1,433 → 1,434 (delta from fd90.138)","description":"fd90.74-150 audit. README test breakdown table at line 919 lists fp-frame at 1,433 unit tests, but after fd90.138 added df_where_mask_df_other_with_dataframe_replacement the actual count is 1,434. Verified:

  find crates/fp-frame -name '*.rs' -exec grep -hE '^\s*#\[test\]|^\s*#\[tokio::test\]|^\s*#\[rstest\]' {} + | wc -l
  → 1434

This is a self-correction — fd90.138 / fd90.139 added the test as part of the where_mask_df_other API symmetry work. Bumping the table cell to match.

Pure README change. +{"id":"frankenpandas-15vsk","title":"fd90.247: Cover more StringAccessor methods (case/find/predicates/cat)","description":"fd90.247: Cover more StringAccessor methods (casefold/swapcase/cat/find/rfind/isalnum/etc.).

fd90.194 covered 26 StringAccessor methods. README claims 50+. Missing:

Case (2 of 6 in README): casefold, swapcase
Search: find, rfind, index_of, rindex_of
Concatenation: cat, cat_list, cat_series
Count: count, count_literal, count_matches (already covered)
Predicates: isalnum, isdecimal, islower, isnumeric, isspace, istitle, isupper
Other: lstrip_chars, rstrip_chars, ljust, rjust, partition, rpartition, normalize, get_dummies, extractall

Scope:
- Extend readme_string_accessor_compiles_and_runs with the next ~15 of the most user-relevant.

 {"id":"frankenpandas-1d1gm","title":"fd90.196: Integration test for README Serialization section (serde_json round-trip)","description":"fd90.196: Add integration test for README "Serialization and Interoperability" section (lines 1560-1580).

The README claims all core types round-trip perfectly through serde_json and that the Scalar enum uses tagged representation. Today no integration test exercises this — the claim could silently break if a derive is dropped or a tag attribute is altered.

Scope:
- Add serde_json as a dev-dependency to crates/frankenpandas/Cargo.toml
- New test: readme_serialization_compiles_and_runs that round-trips:
  - Scalar (each major variant: Bool, Int64, Float64, Utf8, Null) — verify the documented {"kind":..., "value":...} tagged form
  - IndexLabel
  - DataFrame (full round-trip — exercises Index + Column + ValidityMask)
- Verify the round-trip is value-preserving (semantic_eq or PartialEq on outputs)

bincode is mentioned but the README hedges with "or messagepack, etc." — leaving bincode coverage out of scope for this bead to keep dev-dep additions minimal.

Files: crates/frankenpandas/Cargo.toml + crates/frankenpandas/tests/readme_quick_example.rs.
Time estimate: 25-35min.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"test","created_at":"2026-04-26T20:08:14.788817068Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T20:11:17.344972892Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T20:11:17.342961189Z","close_reason":"Added readme_serialization_compiles_and_runs covering serde_json round-trip for Scalar (5 variants — Bool/Int64/Float64/Utf8/Null with assertion that JSON contains 'kind' tag), IndexLabel (Int64 + Utf8), and DataFrame (full round-trip). FOUND AND FIXED A REAL DEFECT: DataFrame.column_order had #[serde(skip)] which silently dropped column ordering on round-trip — directly contradicting the README's 'Round-trips perfectly' claim. Removed the skip and added #[serde(default)] for backwards-compat. Added serde_json as dev-dep. All 1434 fp-frame lib tests + 34 integration + 7 frankenpandas lib + 2 doctests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
